name: Military Coup d'État
target: Civilian government of Aung San Suu Kyi
description: Senior General Min Aung Hlaing seized power, detaining State Counsellor Aung San Suu Kyi and President Win Myint, nullifying the November 2020 election results.

name: Spring Revolution Mass Protests
casualties: Over 1,000 protesters killed by year's end
description: Millions joined nationwide protests and a Civil Disobedience Movement. Security forces responded with lethal force.

name: National Unity Government Established
description: Ousted lawmakers and ethnic leaders formed the National Unity Government as a parallel administration, declaring the junta illegitimate.

name: People's Defence Force Established
description: The NUG announced formation of the People's Defence Force as its armed wing, marking the transition from peaceful protest to organized armed resistance.

name: Sagaing Region Scorched Earth Campaign
casualties: Hundreds killed, thousands of structures burned
description: The Tatmadaw conducted systematic arson and mass killing across Sagaing Region, burning hundreds of villages to crush PDF resistance strongholds.

name: Aung San Suu Kyi Sentenced to 27 Years
description: The junta court convicted Suu Kyi on multiple charges including corruption, sentencing her to 27 years in proceedings widely condemned as politically motivated.

name: Junta Airstrikes on Pazi Gyi Village
casualties: Over 170 killed including women and children
description: Tatmadaw jets bombed a community gathering, killing over 170 people in one of the deadliest single attacks of the civil war.

name: Karenni Resistance Advances on Loikaw
description: Karenni resistance forces tightened their siege around Loikaw, controlling most surrounding territory and restricting junta supply lines.

name: Operation 1027 Launched
target: Junta positions across northern Shan State
casualties: Hundreds of junta troops killed or captured
description: The Three Brotherhood Alliance launched a coordinated offensive across northern Shan State, rapidly overrunning dozens of outposts in the most significant advance since the coup.

name: Fall of Laukkai to MNDAA
target: Laukkai, capital of Kokang Self-Administered Zone
description: MNDAA captured Laukkai after a prolonged siege. Over 2,000 soldiers surrendered — the first fall of a regional capital to resistance forces since the coup.

name: China-Mediated Ceasefire in Northern Shan
description: China brokered a ceasefire in northern Shan State to protect Chinese border interests. The ceasefire froze territorial gains but did not hold in all areas.

name: Junta Activates Conscription Law
description: The junta activated the dormant 2010 conscription law amid severe troop shortages, triggering mass exodus of young people to border areas.

name: Arakan Army Captures Ann Town
description: The Arakan Army captured Ann town and its military base as part of its broader campaign to seize all of Rakhine territory.

name: Battle of Myawaddy Phase 1
description: Karen resistance forces launched an offensive on Myawaddy, Myanmar's major trade gateway to Thailand. Intense fighting with junta and Border Guard Force units.

name: Arakan Army Captures Maungdaw
description: The Arakan Army captured Maungdaw near the Bangladesh border, a town central to the Rohingya crisis, raising questions about Rohingya protection under AA governance.

name: Arakan Army Captures Buthidaung
description: The AA took Buthidaung with intense fighting causing massive Rohingya displacement. Accusations of arson and ethnic targeting accompanied the battle.

name: Capture of Nawnghkio
description: TNLA forces seized Nawnghkio, a key junction on the Mandalay-Lashio corridor, cutting a major junta supply line to the northeast.

name: KIA Captures Hpakant Jade Mines
description: The Kachin Independence Army captured the world's largest jadeite source, a critical revenue stream worth billions annually for the Tatmadaw and connected oligarchs.

name: Fall of Kyaukme
description: TNLA captured Kyaukme, a strategic point along the Mandalay-Lashio highway, further isolating junta positions in northern Shan State.

name: Capture of Hsipaw
description: TNLA captured Hsipaw, consolidating control over the central Shan State corridor between Mandalay and Lashio.

name: Capture of Thabeikkyin
description: Resistance forces captured Thabeikkyin on the Irrawaddy River north of Mandalay, bringing armed resistance closer to Myanmar's second-largest city.

name: Fall of Lashio to TNLA
target: Lashio, HQ of Tatmadaw Northeast Command
description: TNLA-led forces captured Lashio, the largest city in northern Shan State and headquarters of the Tatmadaw's Northeast Regional Military Command — the most strategically significant resistance victory of the war.

name: Arakan Army Controls Most of Rakhine State
description: By late 2024 the Arakan Army controlled virtually all of Rakhine except capital Sittwe, establishing parallel governance and functioning as a de facto state authority.

name: Devastating Mandalay Region Earthquake
description: A magnitude 7.7 earthquake struck central Myanmar, killing thousands. The junta's inadequate relief and obstruction of international aid drew widespread condemnation.

name: Junta Announces Sham Election Plan
description: The junta announced elections under a system designed to favor military-aligned parties, with most opposition banned and vast territory outside junta control.

name: Min Aung Hlaing Assumes Presidency
description: Min Aung Hlaing assumed presidency following sham elections, transitioning from military chief to nominal civilian leader while retaining full military command. Rejected by the NUG and international community.

name: Final Capture of Myawaddy
target: Full control of Myawaddy border town
description: Karen resistance forces completed capture of Myawaddy, Myanmar's most important trade crossing with Thailand, severing a critical junta revenue and supply line.
